source.title: Hippocampal theta sequences reflect current goals

confidence_rationale: Wikenheiser and Redish show that hippocampal theta sequences (awake planning sequences during locomotion) are modulated by current goal -- sequences project further ahead when animals are farther from their goal and compress near the goal. This establishes that online hippocampal sequence content is goal-sensitive. Relevance to SD-017 is indirect: it establishes that hippocampal trajectory representations encode goal-directed structure during the waking period, which is the input that the offline consolidation pipeline (SD-017) must process. However, the paper does not demonstrate necessity of offline consolidation -- it is about online theta sequences during behaviour, not SWR replay during sleep. This is mixed evidence: it supports the architectural claim that hippocampus encodes goal-relevant sequential structure (required for SD-017's SWS-analog replay to be meaningful), but does not speak to whether offline phases are necessary.
failure_signatures: The study is about awake theta sequences during navigation, not offline SWR replay during sleep or rest.
Goal-modulated theta sequences are a waking phenomenon; their necessity for offline consolidation (the core SD-017 claim) is not addressed.
The study does not test what happens to goal-directed behaviour when offline consolidation is blocked.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal theta sequences during locomotion dynamically represent future paths toward the current goal. Sequence length and content are modulated by goal distance -- sequences project further ahead when the animal is further from the goal and compress near it.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-017 requires that the offline SWS-analog and REM-analog phases process content that was encoded during waking. Wikenheiser and Redish establish that the waking input to be consolidated is already goal-structured: hippocampal representations during active navigation carry goal-relative trajectory content. This means the SWS-analog replay phase in SD-017 is replaying sequences that already encode goal-relevant structure -- not just geometric position. For REE's attribution pipeline: the theta sequences carry goal-approach trajectories into the waking hippocampal buffer; the SWS-analog replay then transfers this structured content to the schema layer. Without the waking goal structure, there would be nothing goal-relevant to consolidate. This paper supports the waking encoding half of the consolidation pipeline that SD-017 depends on.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Evidence is mixed because this does not address offline necessity. It is compatible with SD-017 but does not provide necessity evidence. The mapping is to the waking side of the input to the offline consolidation pipeline, not to the offline pipeline itself.
mapping.source_context: Adult rats; T-maze with multiple goal locations; CA1 tetrode recordings during active navigation; theta sequence decoding from population activity; goal-modulation analysis of decoded sequence content and extent.

confidence_components.notes: Nature Neuroscience, Redish lab (expert in hippocampal planning). High source quality. Mapping fidelity lower because the study addresses waking encoding rather than offline necessity. The mixed direction reflects this: the paper supports the architecture that SD-017 operates on, but not the necessity claim.
